steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The poem says the speaker "rests deep within the wood, Drawn by its silent call." The word "drawn" must fit a context where nature's silent call is pulling or luring the speaker into the woods. Look for the choice that captures being attracted or pulled toward something.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Unnoticed': means not seen or overlooked. Nothing in the poem suggests the speaker is invisible or ignored. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Depicted': means shown or represented visually, as in a drawing or painting. The speaker is a person in the woods, not a picture of one. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Enticed': means attracted or tempted toward something. Nature's "silent call" is exactly the kind of lure that entices someone — the speaker is drawn in by it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Envisioned': means imagined or pictured in the mind. The speaker is physically in the wood, not imagining it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
